retry_reason: Failure due to private_buffer is permanent; failure from drm_gem_dmabuf_export (e.g., memory allocation) is not indicated as retryable by the wrapper and typically not retried by callers.
contract_summary: This function exports a DRM GEM object as a dma-buf. It either acquires an additional reference on an already-exported dma_buf (via get_dma_buf) or creates a new export (via drm_gem_dmabuf_export, which initializes the refcount of the GEM object and device). The caller receives a dma_buf with one reference and must call dma_buf_put when done. The function fails with -EOPNOTSUPP if the buffer is marked private (not exportable). Caller must check the return value for errors.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to manage dma-buf reference: either bump refcount on existing dma_buf via get_dma_buf, or create a new one via drm_gem_dmabuf_export (which initializes refcounts). The only minor non-refcount logic is the early private_buffer check and exp_info setup, which are necessary scaffolding.
judge.retry_reason: Failure due to private_buffer is permanent; failure from drm_gem_dmabuf_export (e.g., memory allocation) is not indicated as retryable by the wrapper and typically not retried by callers.

check.reason: The first candidate correctly captures the refcount increment on the gem object (gobj) via drm_gem_dmabuf_export. The second candidate incorrectly uses parameter 1 (gobj) as the starting object for a refcount on the drm_device; the critical variable for that get operation is the device itself (gobj->dev), which is not a direct interface of amdxdna_gem_prime_export, so it is rejected.
